steps.title: Express surface area formulas
steps.content:
A rectangular prism with square base of side $s$ and height $h$ has surface area $2s^2 + 4sh$.

For each given prism with height 90 cm:
$$K = 2s^2 + 4s(90) = 2s^2 + 360s = 2s(s + 180)$$
When two prisms are glued along a square base, they form a prism of height 180 cm:
$$2s^2 + 4s(180) = 2s^2 + 720s = 2s(s + 360)$$

steps.title: Set up and simplify the ratio equation
steps.content:
The combined surface area is $\frac{92}{47}K$, so:
$$\frac{2s(s + 360)}{2s(s + 180)} = \frac{92}{47}$$
Factor out $2s$ and cancel:
$$\frac{s + 360}{s + 180} = \frac{92}{47}$$

steps.title: Solve for the side length
steps.content:
Cross multiply:
$$47(s + 360) = 92(s + 180)$$
$$47s + 16920 = 92s + 16560$$
$$360 = 45s$$
$$s = 8$$
That matches the correct answer — B is correct.
